visual_findings.control_map_diagnostic: The diagnostic image is the same OpenPose skeleton map used by v1 and v2, with no unexpected content.
visual_findings.face_realism: Pass. Face, eyes, hair, expression, skin tone, and lighting are coherent.
visual_findings.control_map_leakage: Pass. No visible OpenPose skeleton lines, colored bones, dots, or pose-map stains appear in the generated image.
visual_findings.framing_change: Partial improvement. The taller 768x1024 request produced a visibly taller composition and brought one hand/forearm into the frame, unlike v2.
visual_findings.hand_quality: Partial improvement with limitations. One hand/forearm is visible and largely plausible, with acceptable wrist/forearm continuity for local iteration. The requested two separated hands on a tabletop are still missing.
visual_findings.tabletop_contact: Fail for the robustness target. No table surface or two-hand tabletop contact is visible.
visual_findings.prompt_compliance: Mixed. Anti-close-up/taller framing improved the result, but the main target of two separated hands on a tabletop remains unmet.

defects.description: The generated image includes one plausible hand/forearm but does not show both separated hands or tabletop contact.

notes: V3 is a material improvement over v2 for visible hand/forearm presence.

Further prompt-only retries against this same head/upper-body OpenPose map are unlikely to certify two-hand tabletop contact; the next useful step is a new control source/control map that explicitly contains both hands and table contact.
promotion_boundary: Local robustness iteration only. It does not certify OpenPose hands/tabletop contact, full-body pose, or target-runtime behavior.
next_action: Prepare or select a new OpenPose control input that explicitly includes both hands and table contact before rerunning this robustness target.
